meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship functional, professional apps in record time.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project stagnation is 'blank canvas syndrome.' A SaaS starter kit provides a pre-configured architecture, authentication, and database schema, allowing you to skip repetitive boilerplate. By leveraging AI-assisted tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform hours of setup into minutes of high-impact feature development.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the client brief is signed, the excitement is high, but three days later, you’re still wrestling with database migrations and authentication middleware. The momentum dies, the code becomes a tangled mess, and your profit margin evaporates into the abyss of 'unfinished projects.'

It doesn't have to be this way. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a high-velocity architect isn't just talent—it’s the leverage of an opinionated, battle-tested starter kit. By offloading the infrastructure to modern AI tools, you reclaim your focus for the features that actually pay the bills.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a consistent workspace architecture to ensure every project starts with a professional directory structure and essential dependencies pre-installed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS starter architecture including Next.js, Supabase auth, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure strict TypeScript configuration and a clean folder structure for modular feature development.

steps.title: Configure Authentication Middleware
steps.description: Implement secure, session-based authentication early so you never have to revisit the login flow once the project scales.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate secure authentication middleware that handles session verification, route protection, and redirects for unauthenticated users in a Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup auth middleware with supabase'

steps.title: Define Database Schemas
steps.description: Use AI to generate normalized database models that prevent technical debt and ensure rapid prototyping of user-facing data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including tables for Users, Organizations, Subscriptions, and Projects with appropriate foreign key relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate prisma schema and push to database'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing
steps.description: Integrate payments immediately to validate your SaaS business model before spending weeks on non-monetizable features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Stripe webhook handler and checkout session endpoint. Ensure it handles successful payments and updates the subscription status in the database.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Automate the CI/CD pipeline to ensure that every push to the main branch is production-ready and stress-tested.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel, including environment variable injection for production keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure github actions for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always store your 'Golden Boilerplate' in a private GitHub template repository so you can spin up a new project in seconds.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth logic before you write the UI; it prevents 90% of production bugs.
Keep your database schema strictly decoupled from your UI components to allow for rapid pivoting when a client changes requirements.
Automate your environment variable management using `.env.example` files to avoid 'it works on my machine' syndrome during handoffs.
Treat your starter kit as a living product: every time you solve a recurring problem for a client, refactor that solution into your core kit.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, focus on building one single 'killer feature' that differentiates your client's product from the competition. Use the time saved on boilerplate to polish the UX.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the setup and start delivering the value. Mastering your workflow is the ultimate competitive advantage in the freelance market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
